data.title: INVO Fertility (IVF) stock falls 14.78% to $2.02 after-hours, up 191.73% during regular sessions. Acquired Family Beginnings clinic for $750,000.
data.description: Healthcare company INVO Fertility Inc. (NASDAQ: IVF) saw its shares experience a significant fluctuation on Wednesday. After closing the regular trading session at $2.37, up 191.73%, the Florida-based company's stock dropped by 14.78% to $2.02 in after-hours trading on Tuesday. The reason behind this sudden movement remains unclear. It is worth noting that on January 15, INVO Fertility, formerly known as NAYA Biosciences, took part in the Lytham Partners 2026 Investor Healthcare Summit, where discussions on company performance and sector outlook were held. Furthermore, the company recently finalized an acquisition agreement with Family Beginnings, P.C., an Indiana-based fertility clinic, for a purchase price including $350,000 in cash payable at closing. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Investors poured $3.2B into Bitcoin in 9 days. This activity could indicate a positive long-term trend, per Santiment.
data.description: Santiment's crypto sentiment platform has noted a significant increase in Bitcoin purchases made by a specific group of holders. This uptick in buying activity suggests a possible "long-term bullish divergence" in the market.

data.title: HistoIndex partners with Houston Research Institute to enhance MASH diagnostic services for chronic liver disease.
data.description: A recent development in Singapore on January 20, 2026, revealed that HistoIndex, an innovative company specializing in AI-powered digital pathology solutions for chronic liver disease, has forged a key partnership with the esteemed Houston Research Institute (HRI). This collaboration is significant as HRI is renowned for its expertise in executing clinical trials, enhancing the potential for groundbreaking advancements in the field. This marks a pivotal moment in the realm of liver disease research, leveraging cutting-edge technology and research capabilities.

data.title: Honda and Princeton NuEnergy sign MOU to collaborate on next-gen battery recycling tech in Princeton, N.J.
data.description: On January 5, 2026, Honda Motor Co., Ltd. ("Honda") and Princeton NuEnergy Inc. ("PNE") unveiled a memorandum of understanding (MOU) in Princeton, New Jersey. This MOU signifies their intent to explore cooperation in the development of advanced lithium-ion battery recycling technologies. This collaboration aims to enhance sustainability efforts in the automotive industry.

data.title: Trump Jr. to host World Liberty Forum at Mar-a-Lago, gathering influential figures in tech and finance on Feb. 18.
data.description: Donald Trump Jr., co-founder of World Liberty Financial (WLFI), revealed plans for the World Liberty Forum during a recent announcement. The forum, set to take place at Mar-a-Lago next month, will convene key figures from the technology and finance sectors. The event, slated for February 18th, was promoted by Trump Jr. as the "most exclusive" gathering in these industries. Described as a meeting of leading individuals in finance and technology, the forum aims to bring together a limited number of highly esteemed professionals responsible for significant financial investments and innovative company developments. Find the complete article on the Benzinga.com website.

data.title: Centerra Gold's Mount Milligan Mine in BC approved for operations until 2035; 10% plant expansion, expedited permit.
data.description: Centerra Gold Inc. has announced that its Mount Milligan Mine in central British Columbia has been granted an amended environmental assessment and all necessary permits to operate until 2035. The authorizations also include a 10% increase in plant throughput starting in 2028 and expanded stockpile capacity for plant feed flexibility. In January 2025, Mount Milligan was chosen by the Province of British Columbia as one of four mining projects eligible for expedited permitting to boost economic growth in the region.
